title: Windows Update Failed During Enrollment
description: Detects a Windows quality/cumulative update that FAILED to install during the OOBE / ESP enrollment window (WindowsUpdateClient EventID 20). A cumulative update failing mid-OOBE is a known cause of enrollment errors and is otherwise invisible in the Intune console.
explanation: A Windows Update failed to install during enrollment: **{{updateTitle}}** (error {{hresult}} {{hresultSymbol}}). Windows can install quality/cumulative updates during OOBE (the "Getting updates" screen and the ESP "Install Windows quality updates during OOBE" option). When one of these fails mid-enrollment it can leave the device in an inconsistent state, force an unexpected reboot, or block the Enrollment Status Page — a failure mode that is otherwise only discoverable by deep manual digging on the device.

remediation.title: Investigate the failed update
steps: Decode the HRESULT ({{hresult}}) against the Windows Update error reference to identify the failure class (WU agent, CBS/servicing, or network).
Check whether the same KB fails on other devices — a broadly failing cumulative update points at a servicing-stack or content issue rather than a single device.
Consider whether Windows Update during OOBE should be deferred (Autopilot ESP setting / update ring) until the problematic update is superseded.
Confirm the device reached the desktop despite the failure; a failed OOBE update does not always fail the enrollment but frequently correlates with ESP delays.
